$comment: Shared, committed Claude Code config so every checkout gets the same safety posture and pre-approved permissions. Machine-specific overrides go in the gitignored .claude/settings.local.json. deny takes precedence over allow, so these blocks hold even under --dangerously-skip-permissions (used by ops/gentriduck-devmode.sh on the native Linux automation host). The 'git push ... main' deny rules are DEFENCE-IN-DEPTH only (ADR-0011): they match 'main' as a ref token (not the substring, so 'domain'/'maintenance' branch names are safe), but exotic forms like 'git push origin HEAD' on a checked-out main, or '--mirror', are NOT caught. main's real protection is the denied gh pr merge + the PM being instructed to only ever touch develop; full enforcement needs a separate PM identity (ADR-0011 Alternative D).
